danger_signals.visual_form: Opponent's health meter visibly decreasing during combat
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates player is taking damage and risk of round loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Opponent launched into air after uppercut, defenseless animation
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent is vulnerable and at immediate critical risk of combo damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct visual effect when special move is performed (character-specific animations)
danger_signals.meaning: High-damage attack incoming; danger to blocking/retreating player

danger_signals.visual_form: Character falls to ground, health bar empty
danger_signals.meaning: Round lost; triggers Fatality vulnerability phase

danger_signals.visual_form: Boss character model distinct from tournament fighters, larger/more imposing design
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated danger; progression to higher-difficulty opponent

safety_signals.visual_form: Spatial separation between characters; range-dependent attack variation
safety_signals.meaning: Player can control engagement distance, reducing incoming damage

safety_signals.visual_form: Character assumes defensive stance when block button held
safety_signals.meaning: Incoming damage reduced; minimal ground loss; counterattack opportunity

safety_signals.visual_form: Opponent's health meter shows substantial remaining capacity
safety_signals.meaning: Safety margin; opponent far from defeat

safety_signals.visual_form: Round timer visible; higher health when time expires
safety_signals.meaning: Win condition met by timeout; player ahead in round

safety_signals.visual_form: Round transition, health bars reset for new round in match
safety_signals.meaning: Player health regenerated; fresh start opportunity after endurance damage

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Player characters (Johnny Cage, Sonya Blade, Liu Kang, etc.)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Earthrealm warriors use standard martial arts clothing and armor; distinct from otherworldly or corrupted designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament arena stages on Shang Tsung's Island; Eastern martial arts aesthetic in stage design

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Goro: multi-armed, reptilian/demonic appearance; Shang Tsung: sorcerer robes, supernatural aura; visually distinct from human fighters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Island tournament setting under Shang Tsung's control; implies otherworldly/corrupted realm influence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Seven tournament stages depict Shang Tsung's Island as hostile arena; fighters face escalating opposition culminating in Goro and Shang Tsung
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must defeat increasing difficulty of opponents; endurance matches force player to fight multiple opponents without health regen between fights

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Goro presented as sub-boss with distinctive monstrous design; appears as penultimate threat before final boss
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Goro fight is mandatory progression checkpoint; signals player approaching climactic confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shang Tsung appears as final boss; tournament stages exist within his domain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final match against Shang Tsung; defeating him resolves the narrative threat to Earthrealm

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mirror match against clone of player character; visual parity indicates player skill now equals their own reflected ability
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mirror match serves as checkpoint; player must overcome a duplicate of themselves, testing mastery of character

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Test Your Might minigame with escalating material difficulty (wood → stone → steel → ruby → diamond)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Optional bonus stages reward player for demonstrating raw power; materials represent ascending challenge tiers

environmental_storytelling.example: Seven Shang Tsung Island locations serve as distinct tournament stages; each stage visual implies different areas of a corrupted realm under sorcerer control

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character visual archetypes communicate faction and role
environmental_storytelling.example: Earthrealm fighters depicted with martial arts uniforms/traditional armor; Goro and Shang Tsung use supernatural/monstrous visual language to signal otherworldly threat
Tournament opponents share similar visual/mechanical grounding; Goro's multi-armed reptilian form and Shang Tsung's sorcerer aesthetic visually signal narrative escalation

environmental_storytelling.technique: Health bar as narrative tension meter
environmental_storytelling.example: Depleting opponent health bar visualizes gaining dominance in tournament; full player health bar signals narrative safety before endurance match gauntlet

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Triggers Fatality finishing move prompt; character executes gruesome finishing animation indicating dominance
Enters defenseless juggle state; cannot block or attack; vulnerable to combo chain
Assumes defensive stance; takes reduced damage; minimal knockback; maintains ground position
Attacks vary by range and distance; perform special moves contextually; mirror match opponent mimics player inputs
Distinct attack patterns and superior damage output; serves as skill gate before final confrontation
Represents ultimate narrative threat; defeat required to resolve Earthrealm's siege by Shang Tsung's forces

notes: Mortal Kombat (1993 Genesis port) uses fighting game mechanical visual language to communicate world-state. The tournament structure itself is the primary narrative scaffold—player progression through increasingly difficult opponents (7 tournament fighters → Mirror Match → Endurance matches → Goro → Shang Tsung) visually manifests Earthrealm's peril. Boss characters (Goro, Shang Tsung) are distinguished through visual design (monstrous/supernatural aesthetic) rather than color schemes or sigils. Safety/danger signals are mechanics-driven: health bars, block states, and airborne vulnerability communicate tactical threat in real-time. Environmental storytelling relies on stage design and character visual archetypes rather than explicit narrative cutscenes. The Fatality system itself functions as a visual reward for victory and reinforces the high-stakes tournament's lethal nature.
